objectives_covered: Define a scalar in plain language as a single number that captures exactly one measurable property.
Identify scalar values in everyday AI contexts (temperature setting, confidence score, pixel brightness, learning rate, loss value).
Explain why scalars are the simplest unit of numerical data (magnitude: size/amount, no direction).
Distinguish what a scalar is (one number) from what it is not (a list of numbers — that is a vector).
Recognise scalars when reading about AI systems — loss, learning rate, confidence score, pixel brightness as scalar roles.

questions.objective_ref: Define a scalar in plain language as a single number that captures exactly one measurable property.
Identify scalar values in everyday AI contexts (learning rate, pixel brightness, confidence score).
Explain what magnitude means for a scalar (size or amount, no direction or structure).
Distinguish what a scalar is (one number) from what it is not — a vector groups several scalars in an ordered list.
Recognise scalar roles in AI systems — loss as a single number summarising model error.

length_bias_check.method: correct answer length vs mean distractor length ratio checked per question; all ratios < 1.20
